cve.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists when the Windows font library improperly handles specially crafted embedded fonts, aka "Win32k Graphics Remote Code Execution Vulnerability." This affects Windows 7, Microsoft Office, Windows Server 2012 R2, Windows RT 8.1, Windows Server 2008, Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows Server 2012, Windows Server 2016, Windows 8.1, Windows 10, Windows 10 Servers.

attack_hypotheses: An attacker could send a specially crafted email with an embedded font to the Exchange Hybrid Relay server, potentially allowing remote code execution.
preconditions: An attacker could send a specially crafted email with an embedded font to the Exchange Hybrid Relay server, potentially allowing remote code execution.

If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to gain control of the MAIL01 server, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive email data, disruption of email services, and lateral movement within the organization's network.
validation_checks: An attacker could send a specially crafted email with an embedded font to the Exchange Hybrid Relay server, potentially allowing remote code execution.

If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to gain control of the MAIL01 server, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive email data, disruption of email services, and lateral movement within the organization's network.
safety_notes: An attacker could send a specially crafted email with an embedded font to the Exchange Hybrid Relay server, potentially allowing remote code execution.

If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to gain control of the MAIL01 server, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive email data, disruption of email services, and lateral movement within the organization's network.

expert.rationale: The vulnerability has a high CVSS score (8.8) and could allow remote code execution, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive email data and disruption of email services. Remediation is necessary to prevent exploitation and protect the organization's critical assets.
remediation_plan: Apply the latest Windows Server 2016 security updates to patch the Win32k Graphics Remote Code Execution Vulnerability (CVE-2018-8332)
Implement email content filtering to block specially crafted emails with embedded fonts
Configure the Exchange Hybrid Relay server to use a secure font rendering library
compensating_controls: Implement an email gateway with advanced threat protection capabilities to detect and block malicious emails
Configure the server firewall to restrict incoming email traffic to only trusted sources
Monitor server logs for suspicious activity and implement an incident response plan in case of exploitation
follow_up_questions: Apply the latest Windows Server 2016 security updates to patch the Win32k Graphics Remote Code Execution Vulnerability (CVE-2018-8332)

Implement an email gateway with advanced threat protection capabilities to detect and block malicious emails
